title: Salpichlaena adnata
taxonomy: Salpichlaena adnata is a species of plant that belongs to the family Marattiaceae. This family is a part of the division Pteridophyta, which comprises ferns. The class is Polypodiopsida, and the order is Polypodiales. The family Marattiaceae is further divided into several genera, with Salpichlaena being one of them. Salpichlaena adnata is native to the tropical forests of Australia.
uses: Salpichlaena adnata is a species of plant that has been found to have various medicinal uses in traditional medicine. intro: Salpichlaena adnata, also known as the "Ginger Lily" or "Kadukkai", is a type of medicinal plant that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.
